feature: Patch Engine (ADR-074) — transactional protocol for agent-driven code changes
summary: Three-phase transactional protocol (patch_begin → patch_check → patch_commit) for agent-driven code changes. Provides scope validation, diff metrics, forbidden-path detection, and evidence records. Advisory enforcement (tag + warning, no hard block). Available via API (drift.api.patch), MCP tools (drift_patch_begin, drift_patch_check, drift_patch_commit), CLI (drift patch begin|check|commit), and A2A skills. PatchIntent and PatchVerdict Pydantic models expose status enum (clean / review_required / rollback_recommended). TaskSpec.to_patch_intent() and AgentTask.to_patch_intent() bridge methods enable existing task specs to emit patch intents. Session tracks active_patches and patch_history. PolicyCompiler: compile_policy() converts Policy objects to directive sets for agent consumption. Additional fixes: LLM format now respects --max-findings, BaselineManager singleton thread safety via threading.Lock.
design_goals: Three-phase transactional protocol so agents can declare intent, check scope, and commit evidence atomically
Advisory enforcement to avoid blocking legitimate agent-driven repairs
Expose via API, MCP, CLI, and A2A to cover all agent integration surfaces
Reuse existing TaskSpec and AgentTask objects via bridge methods to avoid duplication
Thread-safe BaselineManager singleton to prevent races under concurrent access
non_goals: No changes to scoring weights or thresholds
No hard blocking of patches — advisory enforcement only

self_analysis.notes: No signal logic changed. Patch Engine is an additive transactional layer over the existing repair workflow.
